quotes.text: You want to know the hardest part of a trade negotiation? Sitting across from someone who smiles the entire time they're saying no. I've seen used-car dealers with more tells.
quotes.speaker: Mike Pompeo
quotes.context: Keynote address at the Hudson Institute Annual Dinner, Washington D.C., November 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pompeo's Kansas plain-speaking and his fondness for punchy, slightly boastful storytelling from his diplomatic days make this very plausible. The giveaway is the used-car-dealer line; his real analogies tend to stay closer to military or business imagery.

quotes.text: I am extraordinarily patient, provided I get my own way in the end.
quotes.speaker: Margaret Thatcher
quotes.context: Self-aware, tongue-in-cheek remark by the British Prime Minister in an interview published in The Observer, 4 April 1989 (as catalogued by the Margaret Thatcher Centre's quotes page). Speaker was a UK Conservative Prime Minister.

quotes.text: I've been on their list for a long time. That's what we're dealing with.
quotes.speaker: Donald Trump
quotes.context: Trump speaking to the New York Post about Iran assassination threats

source.title: Politics — Trump 'left instructions' to bomb Iran 'at levels that they've never seen' if he

quotes.text: Somebody once told me I should smile more in debates. I thought about it for a second and decided that was probably advice I could live without.
quotes.speaker: Kamala Harris
quotes.context: Remarks at Emily's List National Gala, Washington D.C., May 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Harris's tendency to call out double standards with a light touch rather than a lecture, and her signature pause before the punchline, feel very true to form. The tell is that in real life she'd be more likely to tie the moment to a broader point about women in politics rather than leaving it hanging as pure wit.

quotes.text: I've been in enough committee hearings to know that the person talking the longest is almost never the person who read the document.
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Warren
quotes.context: Interview on The Axe Files with David Axelrod, podcast episode released October 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Warren's professorial directness and her genuine frustration with Senate theater make this feel completely plausible. The subtle tell is that her real humor tends to come wrapped in a specific anecdote or a constituent's name rather than as a free-standing observation.

quotes.text: I am Al Gore, I used to be the next president of the United States
quotes.speaker: Al Gore
quotes.context: His self-deprecating opening line at a Harvard Kennedy School lecture, October 4, 2001, after losing the 2000 election

source.title: Harvard Gazette — Gore calls for unity
